items.summary: Built LoadSense, an edge-AI + cloud occupancy platform for Philippine jeepneys, with Team FlowerBoys. Placed Top 6 in the Smart Cities track and Top 40 overall.
description: LoadSense uses a YOLOv8-nano model to count boarding and alighting passengers on public utility vehicles, mapping the live count to a four-tier occupancy state shown on a windshield LED strip so waiting commuters can see at a glance whether a jeepney has room.

A FastAPI + SQLite cloud layer ingests GPS and occupancy telemetry to predict ETA, forecast route demand, flag route deviations and driving anomalies, and drives an operator console with a live fleet map (Leaflet) and an alert verification workflow.
bullets: Placed Top 6 in the Smart Cities track and Top 40 overall at ASEAN AI Hackathon 2026
Designed the edge (YOLOv8-nano occupancy counting) and cloud (FastAPI/SQLite) architecture
Built the operator console, live fleet map, and alert verification workflow
achievements: Top 6, Smart Cities track — ASEAN AI Hackathon 2026
Advanced to and competed in the virtual semi-finals

items.organization: HCI Coursework · University of San Jose-Recoletos
items.summary: Leading architecture and design on Hira, a persona-aware Filipino labor-matching platform for blue-collar workers, fresh graduates, and OFWs, built as HCI coursework and drafted into a Design Science Research paper.
description: Hira serves dual purpose as an HCI course deliverable and a potential hackathon entry. The frontend is a Next.js 16 + Tailwind v4 + TypeScript scaffold with a forest-green design system, built frontend-first against mock data with government API integrations (TESDA, SSS, Pag-IBIG, DMW) deferred to a later phase.

The design work was written up as an independent paper, "Persona-Aware Platform Design for Cross-Segment Labor Market Integration in the Philippines," with plans to adapt it into competition proposals.
bullets: Led architecture and persona-driven design across employer, job-seeker, and government-agency views
Directed a full Figma design build alongside the coded frontend
Drafted an independent Design Science Research paper on the platform's design approach
achievements: Shipped a working frontend demo running entirely on mock data
Completed a full DSR paper draft for independent publication

items.summary: Built a Naive Bayes classifier that flags fraudulent/phishing emails and prompt-injection attempts, with a custom 300-row labeled dataset and a Streamlit app for the final defense.
description: Designed a custom CSV dataset spanning safe, fraudulent, and prompt-injection email samples, then built text-feature extraction and a Naive Bayes classifier around it.
Wrapped the model in a Streamlit app for live demonstration during the course defense.
bullets: Built and labeled a 300-row custom email dataset across three classes
Implemented Naive Bayes classification with custom text-feature extraction
Shipped a Streamlit demo app for the finals defense
achievements: Completed as a solo finals project for Intelligent Systems

items.summary: Studying Computer Science with a specialization in AI/ML, working across HCI, data analytics, algorithms, and IP/privacy law coursework alongside a small competition team, FlowerBoys.
description: Coursework has spanned data analytics (correlation analysis, exploratory notebooks), AI surveillance and biometrics policy (PhilSys, NPC, Clearview AI, BIPA), and SIPP topics on IP rights and privacy/data rights.
Outside of class requirements, has built and competed with hackathon projects (LoadSense) and independent research write-ups (Hira) alongside team FlowerBoys.
bullets: Specializing in AI/ML within the BSCS program
Core member of competition team FlowerBoys
Coursework spans HCI, data analytics, algorithms, and SIPP (IP/privacy law)
achievements: Top 6, Smart Cities track, ASEAN AI Hackathon 2026 (with FlowerBoys)

heading: Experience
subtitle: A running record of coursework, hackathons, and the projects that came out of them.
